title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Andrew Jagg Tewell, a school principal, explains to Ellery and the others the history of a Croatian blood feud that forced his family to flee Montenegro for America. To escape a man named Croate (also referred to as Crowsack or Croscack), the three remaining family members—Andrew, Stephen Megalos, and the deceased Tom—sold their property, assumed fake names, and adopted different nationalities. While Tom and Stephen believed Croate was merely a myth, Andrew lived in constant fear of his return.

To protect his identity, Andrew created a double personality by impersonating a mountain man named "Old Pete." He maintained an abandoned shack in the mountains and used disguise supplies to appear as a recluse in the town of Arroyo during his spare time.

On Christmas morning, while living as Old Pete in his mountain shack, Andrew discovered a mutilated corpse nailed to a T-shaped cross at a local intersection. He realized that Croate had mistaken a man named Kline for Andrew and had killed him. To protect his own identity, Andrew decided to let Croate believe he had successfully killed him. He swapped Kline's personal items with Andrew's own belongings to ensure the body would be identified as the principal. After burning Kline's items in a forest, Andrew waited for a farmer, Michael O'Keefe, to arrive so he could "discover" the body with a witness. Before heading to town, Andrew sent a letter to Tom warning him of Croate's revenge and stating that, for his own safety, Andrew Van was now dead and only Old Pete remained.
